researchQuestion: What are the biggest risks hiding in Uniswap's onchain data?

tables.name: Daily Volume by Chain
tables.description: Tracks daily trading volume across all Uniswap deployments by blockchain network.

tables.name: TVL Breakdown by Pool
tables.description: Total value locked in top Uniswap V3 pools, updated daily.

tables.name: Whale Movements
tables.description: Tracks swaps above $1M USD value in the last 30 days.

tables.name: LP Fee Revenue
tables.description: Daily fee revenue earned by liquidity providers across all chains.

tables.name: New Pool Deployments
tables.description: Tracks new V3 pool creation events with initial liquidity.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Welcome to DataBard! Today we're diving into Uniswap's onchain analytics — 6 Dune queries, health score 61 out of 100. There's a real story in this data.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Right away I see problems. One query has zero results, another is missing a description entirely. And the data freshness is concerning — let's dig in.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Let's start with the good news. Daily Volume by Chain tracks 2,190 data points across all Uniswap deployments. The data ranges from $150 million to $5.2 billion per day — with Ethereum leading at roughly 78% of total volume.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: The volume column shows an average of $2.3 billion daily. But that range — $150M to $5.2B — is extremely volatile. Any dashboard relying on this needs to account for that variance.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: TVL Breakdown is solid — 500 pools tracked with fee tiers and token pairs. The top values show USDC-ETH and WBTC-ETH dominating. This is the kind of data LPs actually care about.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Now here's a red flag. Whale Movements — tracking swaps above $1 million — has zero results. The query ran, but there's no data. Either the threshold is wrong or the query logic is broken.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: And the freshness shows May 4th — two days stale. For a whale alert query, that's a serious gap. Big trades could be happening unnoticed.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Gas Cost Analysis has results — 365 days of data — but it's missing a description. Nobody knows what this query measures unless they read the SQL. Average gas is showing 0.003 ETH per swap, with about 2.1% failed swaps.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: LP Fee Revenue is queued but hasn't executed yet. We can't tell what the fee trends look like. That's a blind spot for anyone evaluating LP returns.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: New Pool Deployments is healthy — 8,200 pools created with initial liquidity tracked. The top tokens show a long tail of memecoins getting pools deployed. Good data, well-documented.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Bottom line: Uniswap's volume and TVL data is strong, but whale tracking is broken and gas analysis needs documentation. Health score 61 — there's work to do.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Priority one: fix the Whale Movements query — it's returning zero results. Priority two: add a description to Gas Cost Analysis. Priority three: execute the LP Fee Revenue query. Until next time.
